examples.title: Hello world
examples.notes: Implemented hello-world example with deterministic reference artefacts and checked-in user validation evidence from a successful Pythonista runner run.

examples.title: Live logs and progress
examples.notes: Implemented live log streaming example with deterministic reference artefacts and checked-in user validation evidence.

examples.title: Process input files
examples.notes: Implemented bundled CSV processing example with deterministic reference artefacts and checked-in user validation evidence. The deterministic CSV reference was corrected after user validation exposed a newline mismatch.

examples.title: Cancel long-running job
examples.notes: Implemented long-running cancellation example with signal handling and partial progress outputs. A completion-path user run is checked in, but the intended cancellation-path validation is still pending.

examples.title: Optional requirements install
examples.notes: Implemented optional requirements example using a vendored local wheel via requirements.txt, so the success path no longer depends on PyPI access. A failure-path user run with requirements disabled is checked in, plus deterministic success-path reference artefacts. The success-path user run is still pending.

examples.title: Python package and tests
examples.notes: Scaffold placeholder for the richer Python project example.

examples.title: C++ compile and run
examples.notes: Scaffold placeholder for the native C++ example.

examples.title: Rust Cargo CLI
examples.notes: Scaffold placeholder for the Rust Cargo example.

examples.title: Polyglot same-task benchmark
examples.notes: Scaffold placeholder for the benchmark example.

examples.title: Python orchestrates native binary
examples.notes: Scaffold placeholder for the mixed-language orchestration example.

examples.title: Cached per-job requirements
examples.notes: Package-focused example that should be run twice in per_job mode so the second run can demonstrate reusable environment attachment and package cache diagnostics.

examples.title: Offline wheelhouse install
examples.notes: Package-focused example that proves the public wheel upload to private wheelhouse flow without bundling the wheel inside the job zip.

examples.title: Named package profile run
examples.notes: Package-focused example that proves profile mode by attaching a prepared named package profile with no job-local requirements.txt.
